# Basic Example — Non-Production VNet Integration

This example demonstrates the minimum configuration to enable Power Platform VNet injection for
non-production (Sandbox) environments. A single enterprise policy is created in one Azure
subscription, with a primary virtual network.

## Prerequisites

Before applying this example:

1. Deploy [`ptn-environmentgroup`](https://registry.terraform.io/modules/rpothin/ptn-environmentgroup/powerplatform/latest) and note its `environments` output
2. Ensure all environments are **Managed Environments** (prerequisite for enterprise policy linking)
3. Configure OIDC credentials for both Azure and Power Platform providers

## NSG Rules

The `nsg_additional_rules` shown are the **minimum** required for Power Platform VNet injection.
Without outbound allow rules, the built-in `DenyAllOutBound` NSG rule will block PP connectivity.
Adjust priorities and add further rules as needed for your environment.

<!-- markdownlint-disable MD033 -->
## Requirements

The following requirements are needed by this module:

- <a name="requirement_terraform"></a> [terraform](#requirement\_terraform) (>= 1.9, < 2.0)

- <a name="requirement_azapi"></a> [azapi](#requirement\_azapi) (~> 2.0)

- <a name="requirement_azurerm"></a> [azurerm](#requirement\_azurerm) (~> 4.0)

- <a name="requirement_powerplatform"></a> [powerplatform](#requirement\_powerplatform) (~> 4.0)

## Resources

No resources.

<!-- markdownlint-disable MD013 -->
## Required Inputs

The following input variables are required:

### <a name="input_environments"></a> [environments](#input\_environments)

Description: Map of Power Platform environments. Typically the direct output of ptn-environmentgroup.environments.

Type:

```hcl
map(object({
    id            = string
    display_name  = string
    type          = string
    dataverse_url = optional(string)
    location      = string
  }))
```

### <a name="input_subscription_id"></a> [subscription\_id](#input\_subscription\_id)

Description: Azure subscription ID. Used for both non-production and production provider aliases in this single-subscription example.

Type: `string`

## Optional Inputs

The following input variables are optional (have default values):

### <a name="input_enterprise_policy_location"></a> [enterprise\_policy\_location](#input\_enterprise\_policy\_location)

Description: The Power Platform region for the enterprise policy (must match the environment group location).

Type: `string`

Default: `"unitedstates"`

### <a name="input_environment_group_name"></a> [environment\_group\_name](#input\_environment\_group\_name)

Description: The name of the environment group (must match the ptn-environmentgroup deployment).

Type: `string`

Default: `"example-basic"`

### <a name="input_primary_vnet_location"></a> [primary\_vnet\_location](#input\_primary\_vnet\_location)

Description: Azure region for the primary virtual network (e.g. 'eastus', 'westeurope').

Type: `string`

Default: `"eastus"`

### <a name="input_resource_group_location"></a> [resource\_group\_location](#input\_resource\_group\_location)

Description: Azure region for the non-production resource group.

Type: `string`

Default: `"eastus"`

### <a name="input_tags"></a> [tags](#input\_tags)

Description: Tags to apply to all Azure resources.

Type: `map(string)`

Default:

```json
{
  "environment": "non-production",
  "managed_by": "terraform"
}
```

## Outputs

The following outputs are exported:

### <a name="output_enterprise_policy_links"></a> [enterprise\_policy\_links](#output\_enterprise\_policy\_links)

Description: Map of all enterprise policy links keyed by environment key.

### <a name="output_non_production_enterprise_policy_id"></a> [non\_production\_enterprise\_policy\_id](#output\_non\_production\_enterprise\_policy\_id)

Description: The Azure ARM resource ID of the non-production enterprise policy.

### <a name="output_non_production_primary_subnet_id"></a> [non\_production\_primary\_subnet\_id](#output\_non\_production\_primary\_subnet\_id)

Description: The Azure resource ID of the non-production primary PP-delegated subnet.

### <a name="output_non_production_primary_vnet_id"></a> [non\_production\_primary\_vnet\_id](#output\_non\_production\_primary\_vnet\_id)

Description: The Azure resource ID of the non-production primary virtual network.

### <a name="output_non_production_resource_group_name"></a> [non\_production\_resource\_group\_name](#output\_non\_production\_resource\_group\_name)

Description: The name of the non-production Azure resource group.
